title: cosmos bipinnatus var. parviflorus
intro: Cosmos bipinnatus var. parviflorus, also known as the dwarf cosmos or baby cosmos, is a variety of the cosmos bipinnatus plant, a flowering plant in the asteraceae family. It is native to Mexico and is known for its delicate, feathery leaves and small, pink to purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The leaves and flowers of the cosmos bipinnatus var. parviflorus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and have been used to aid in the healing of cuts and scrapes, as well as to reduce fever and alleviate digestive issues.
